name: Bar Canete
summary: A central Barcelona tapas dinner candidate with a reservation link and polished local-product focus.
whyItMatters: After the Montjuic/Barcelona race days, this is a strong destination-meal option that stays in the traditional Spanish/Catalan lane without becoming a tasting-menu project.
pros: Official site has a reservation link
Central address near La Rambla/Raval makes it useful after sightseeing
Official site emphasizes fresh Catalan fish, seafood, seasonal vegetables, and local suppliers
cons: Official page did not expose current opening hours in browsable text
Likely crowded and reservation-sensitive during a major event weekend

logistics.opening_hours: Unknown from official page text; verify on reservation platform.
logistics.reservation_needed: Yes, recommended; official site links to reservations.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until lodging is confirmed; central Barcelona.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Approximately 2-3 km from Montjuic depending on exact viewing point.
logistics.transit: Central city transit/walking; verify race-day disruptions.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No specific closure information found for this business.

timing.best_visit_window: Barcelona dinner after Stage 1 or Stage 2, if access and reservation timing work.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium-high due to event crowds and unknown Sunday hours.

sources.title: Bar Canete official website
sources.note: Supports address, phone, reservation link, restaurant story, and local seafood/produce positioning.

open_questions: Verify July 2026 hours, especially Sunday July 5.
Check access from Montjuic after Stage 2 once official city race operations are published.
